page: Amazon Web Services c7i-flex.large is an x86_64 compute-optimized server powered by an Intel Xeon 8488C processor running at 3.2 GHz. It features 2 vCPUs (1 physical core with 2 threads) and 4.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, yielding a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The processor includes an L3 cache of 105 MB and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX). This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core capabilities, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2067.0 and single-threaded gzip compression reaching 33.49 GB/sec. However, multi-core and memory-intensive operations show limitations. The stress-ng multi-core performance is rated poor at 2086.47, and the Geekbench multi-core score is 2547.0. Memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks is relatively low, measuring 13.13 GB/sec for read and 14.34 GB/sec for write operations.

Given the 4.0 GB memory capacity and lack of local storage, this server is not suited for memory-heavy databases or high-throughput storage tasks, as reflected in its low Passmark database score of 1032.19 and Redis SET throughput of 2.42 million operations per second. It is best utilized for lightweight compute-bound workloads, single-threaded processing tasks, basic web serving, or small-scale utility applications that benefit from modern Intel CPU instructions without requiring massive memory footprints.
description: Amazon Web Services c7i-flex.large is an x86_64 compute-optimized server utilizing an Intel Xeon 8488C processor clocked at 3.2 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it provides 2 vCPUs (1 physical core) and 4.0 GB of DDR5 memory, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks reveal strong single-core execution, highlighted by a Geekbench single-core score of 2067.0 and single-threaded gzip compression of 33.49 GB/sec. Conversely, multi-core and memory-bound benchmarks are weaker, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 2086.47 and uncached read memory bandwidth of 13.13 GB/sec. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for entry-level compute tasks, making it suitable for single-threaded processing, lightweight web serving, and basic utility workloads that do not demand high memory capacity or local storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7i-flex.large is an x86_64 compute-optimized server with 2 vCPUs, 4.0 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, offering strong single-core performance for lightweight workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c7i-flex.large is an x86_64 compute-optimized server featuring 2 vCPUs, 4.0 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: An entry-level compute-optimized instance featuring DDR5 memory and strong single-core performance for lightweight processing workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an Intel Xeon 8488C processor at 3.2 GHz with 2 vCPUs and 4.0 GB DDR5 RAM.
Features Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated vector and matrix operations.
Delivers strong single-core performance, including high single-threaded gzip compression speeds.
Lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage.
Best suited for lightweight single-threaded tasks, basic web serving, and small utility workloads.
